channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PTI urges independent judicial probe into May 9 incidents
item.description: The Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) has once again demanded the creation of an independent judicial commission to conduct a transparent and impartial investigation into the events of May 9, 2023, which...

item.title: Bistrifluron effectively eliminates drywood termite colonies safely
item.description: Researchers at the University of California, Riverside have developed a chemical called bistrifluron that targets drywood termites by disrupting their ability to molt, preventing them from forming new...

item.title: Melbourne Housing Market Shows Signs of Stabilising Amid Affordability and Population Shifts
item.description: Melbourne's housing market has been relatively subdued over the past five years, with median house prices increasing less than in other Australian capitals such as Brisbane, Perth, Canberra, and Adela...

item.title: Bengal Minister Comments on Election Outcome Regarding Mamata Banerjee
item.description: The article, based on the title and context available, appears to report comments made by West Bengal Minister Agnimitra Paul concerning the recent election results in the state. The minister emphasis...

item.title: Frontier Airlines Flight Involved in Fatal Pedestrian Accident at Denver Airport
item.description: A tragic incident occurred at Denver International Airport on Friday morning when a Frontier Airlines Airbus A321 struck and killed a pedestrian who had jumped over the airport perimeter fence and ent...

item.title: Two Dead, One Missing During Treasure Hunt in Bohol Cave
item.description: Two men have tragically died and another remains missing after participating in a treasure hunting activity in Dagohoy, Bohol. The deceased, Marcelo Augis, 57, and Raymundo Valmoria, 35, are believed ...

item.title: Reflections on a Lifelong Bond Between Mother and Daughter
item.description: Christine Kearney reflects on the unique and enduring relationship she shares with her mother, contrasting their personalities and life experiences while emphasising the deep mutual understanding they...

item.title: Caitlin Clark returns from injury with 20-point effort but Indiana Fever fall to Dallas Wings
item.description: Caitlin Clark made her highly anticipated return from a groin injury in the 2026 WNBA season opener as the Indiana Fever hosted the Dallas Wings on May 9. Despite Clark's efforts, the Fever dropped a ...

item.title: Ongoing Gulf clashes show fragile ceasefire between US and Iran as tensions persist
item.description: The conflict between the United States and Iran is still far from ending, even as a ceasefire remains in place. In recent days, there have been renewed clashes in and around the Strait of Hormuz, show...

item.title: NPS clarifies discontinued female recruits were already pregnant before joining Kiganjo training
item.description: The National Police Service (NPS) in Kenya has issued a clarification following public concern and social media claims about 18 female recruits who were discontinued from training at the Kiganjo Polic...

item.title: Stephanie Busari honours resilience as 12 former Chibok girls graduate from AUN
item.description: Veteran journalist Stephanie Busari deliver one emotional commencement speech for American University of Nigeria (AUN) graduation ceremony, where she praise students for their strength and perseveranc...

item.title: Analysis of Vijay’s Potential Chief Ministership in Tamil Nadu and DMK’s Role in Coalition Dynamics
item.description: The political landscape of Tamil Nadu has witnessed considerable speculation over the possibility of popular actor Vijay entering active politics and assuming the role of Chief Minister. TVK Vijay, a ...

item.title: One Nation Wins Farrer Byelection, Liberal Party Faces Leadership Pressure
item.description: The Farrer byelection has delivered a significant blow to the Liberal Party, with One Nation securing its first-ever House of Representatives seat. The result reflects deep dissatisfaction among voter...

item.title: Pakistan Signs $1.2m Contract to Boost US Engagement on Defence and Critical Minerals
item.description: Pakistan has considerably increased its lobbying presence in Washington, signing a $1.2 million, two-year contract with the Ervin Graves Strategy Group LLC. This contract, requiring monthly payments o...

item.title: Nairobi Roads Closure from May 11–12 for African Forward Summit 2026
item.description: Police in Nairobi have announced major traffic disruptions starting Monday, May 11, and continuing through Tuesday, May 12, as the city prepares for the African Forward Summit 2026 at the Kenyatta Int...

item.title: ANC and SACP face potential split ahead of 2026 local elections
item.description: The longstanding alliance between the African National Congress (ANC) and the South African Communist Party (SACP) is entering a period of uncertainty as the SACP prepares to contest the 2026 local go...

item.title: France shifts Africa strategy, targeting new partnerships at Nairobi summit
item.description: France is adjusting its approach in Africa after losing influence in several West African countries, especially in the Sahel region where recent coups have led to the removal of French troops and grow...

item.title: Paul Daley’s First EV Holiday: Navigating Long Drives and Charging Stops
item.description: Guardian Australia columnist Paul Daley embarks on his first holiday driving an electric vehicle amid concerns over fuel security linked to the US-Israel conflict with Iran. The article chronicles his...

item.title: C. Joseph Vijay to be sworn in as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, ending six decades of alternating Dravidian rule
item.description: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) president C. Joseph Vijay is all set to become the Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu on May 10, 2026, marking a historic shift in the state's political landscape after nearly...

item.title: Top tennis players threaten French Open boycott over prize money dispute
item.description: World number one Jannik Sinner has revealed that leading tennis players are seriously considering a boycott of the French Open due to ongoing disagreements over prize money and revenue sharing. The di...
